title: Configuration framework and positioning reference signal (prs) association mechanisms and triggering for reconfigurable intelligent surface (ris)-aided positioning and object sensing
abstract: Disclosed are techniques for wireless positioning. In an aspect, a user equipment (UE) engages in a positioning session or a sensing session and receives, from a network entity, assistance data for at least one positioning reference signal (PRS) resource transmitted by a transmission point, the assistance data including a PRS resource location field indicating a geographic location of the transmission point, the assistance data further including reflection point object (RPO) information for at least one RPO that is capable of reflecting a waveform associated with the at least one PRS resource, the RPO information including at least location information for the at least one RPO.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of wireless positioning performed by a user equipment (UE), comprising: engaging in a positioning session or a sensing session; and receiving, from a network entity, assistance data for at least one positioning reference signal (PRS) resource transmitted by a transmission point, the assistance data including a PRS resource location field indicating a geographic location of the transmission point, the assistance data further including reflection point object (RPO) information for at least one RPO that is capable of reflecting a waveform associated with the at least one PRS resource, the RPO information including at least location information for the at least one RPO. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein a direction of reflections from the at least one RPO is controllable. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the at least one RPO is a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein a direction of reflections from the at least one RPO is not controllable. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the at least one RPO is an RIS not connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs, a mirror, glass, metal, or other reflective object. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the location information for the at least one RPO is a geographic location of the at least one RPO. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein: a geographic location of the at least one RPO is not known to the network entity, and 

 the location information for the at least one RPO is an estimated location of the at least one RPO. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the PRS resource location field includes the geographic location of the transmission point and the location information for the at least one RPO. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein: the RPO information further includes an RPO identifier, and the PRS resource location field includes the geographic location of the transmission point and the RPO identifier. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the RPO information further includes a frequency band, a frequency range, or both in which the at least one RPO operates. 11. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving the RPO information in broadcast information from a base station. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the broadcast information comprises a positioning system information block (PosSIB). 13. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving the RPO information in unicast information from a serving base station. 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising: transmitting a request to the network entity for the RPO information. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein: the RPO is an RIS, and the RIS is associated with a plurality of RPO identifiers, each of the plurality of RPO identifiers associated with a different direction of reflection from the RIS. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein: the RPO is an RIS. and the RIS is associated with a single RPO identifier and a plurality of RPO beam identifiers, each of the plurality of RPO beam identifiers associated with a different direction of reflection from the RIS. 17. The method of claim 1, wherein an association of an RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource is configured statically. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the association of the RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource being configured statically comprises the assistance data being received via a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) positioning protocol (LPP) Provide Assistance Data message. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein an association of an RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource is configured semi-statically. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the association of the RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource being configured semi-statically comprises the assistance data being received via radio resource control (RRC), LPP, or both signaling. 21. The method of claim 1, wherein an association of an RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource is configured dynamically. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the association of the RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource being configured dynamically comprises the assistance data being received via medium access control control element (MAC- CE), downlink control information (DCI), or both. 23. A method of wireless positioning performed by a network entity, comprising: engaging in a positioning session or a sensing session with a user equipment (UE); 

 determining reflection point object (RPO) information for at least one RPO that is capable of reflecting a waveform, the RPO information including at least location information for the at least one RPO; and transmitting the RPO information to the UE. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the at least one RPO is a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs. 25. The method of claim 23, wherein the at least one RPO is an RIS not connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs, a mirror, glass, metal, or other reflective object. 26. The method of claim 23, wherein the location information for the at least one RPO is a geographic location of the at least one RPO. 27. The method of claim 23, wherein: a geographic location of the at least one RPO is not known to the network entity, and the location information for the at least one RPO is an estimated location of the at least one RPO. 28. The method of claim 23, wherein: the RPO information further includes an RPO identifier, and the PRS resource location field includes a geographic location of the at least one RPO and the RPO identifier. 29. A user equipment (UE), comprising: a memory; at least one transceiver; and at least one processor communicatively coupled to the memory and the at least one transceiver, the at least one processor configured to: engage in a positioning session or a sensing session; and 

 receive, via the at least one transceiver, from a network entity, assistance data for at least one positioning reference signal (PRS) resource transmitted by a transmission point, the assistance data including a PRS resource location field indicating a geographic location of the transmission point, the assistance data further including reflection point object (RPO) information for at least one RPO that is capable of reflecting a waveform associated with the at least one PRS resource, the RPO information including at least location information for the at least one RPO. 30. The UE of claim 29, wherein a direction of reflections from the at least one RPO is controllable. 31. The UE of claim 30, wherein the at least one RPO is a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs. 32. The UE of claim 29, wherein a direction of reflections from the at least one RPO is not controllable. 33. The UE of claim 32, wherein the at least one RPO is an RIS not connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs, a mirror, glass, metal, or other reflective object. 34. The UE of claim 29, wherein the location information for the at least one RPO is a geographic location of the at least one RPO. 35. The UE of claim 29, wherein: a geographic location of the at least one RPO is not known to the network entity, and the location information for the at least one RPO is an estimated location of the at least one RPO. 36. The UE of claim 29, wherein the PRS resource location field includes the geographic location of the transmission point and the location information for the at least one RPO. 37. The UE of claim 29, wherein: the RPO information further includes an RPO identifier, and the PRS resource location field includes the geographic location of the transmission point and the RPO identifier. 38. The UE of claim 37, wherein the RPO information further includes a frequency band, a frequency range, or both in which the at least one RPO operates. 39. The UE of claim 29, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: receive, via the at least one transceiver, the RPO information in broadcast information from a base station. 40. The UE of claim 39, wherein the broadcast information comprises a positioning system information block (PosSIB). 41. The UE of claim 29, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: receive, via the at least one transceiver, the RPO information in unicast information from a serving base station. 42. The UE of claim 29, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: cause the at least one transceiver to transmit a request to the network entity for the RPO information. 43. The UE of claim 29, wherein: the RPO is an RIS, and the RIS is associated with a plurality of RPO identifiers, each of the plurality of RPO identifiers associated with a different direction of reflection from the RIS. 44. The UE of claim 29, wherein: 

 the RPO is an RIS. and the RIS is associated with a single RPO identifier and a plurality of RPO beam identifiers, each of the plurality of RPO beam identifiers associated with a different direction of reflection from the RIS. 45. The UE of claim 29, wherein an association of an RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource is configured statically. 46. The UE of claim 45, wherein the association of the RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource being configured statically comprises the assistance data being received via a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) positioning protocol (LPP) Provide Assistance Data message. 47. The UE of claim 29, wherein an association of an RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource is configured semi-statically. 48. The UE of claim 47, wherein the association of the RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource being configured semi-statically comprises the assistance data being received via radio resource control (RRC), LPP, or both signaling. 49. The UE of claim 29, wherein an association of an RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource is configured dynamically. 50. The UE of claim 49, wherein the association of the RPO identifier of the at least one RPO to the at least one PRS resource being configured dynamically comprises the assistance data being received via medium access control control element (MAC-CE), downlink control information (DCI), or both. 51. A network entity, comprising: a memory; at least one transceiver; and 

 at least one processor communicatively coupled to the memory and the at least one transceiver, the at least one processor configured to: engage in a positioning session or a sensing session with a user equipment (UE); determine reflection point object (RPO) information for at least one RPO that is capable of reflecting a waveform, the RPO information including at least location information for the at least one RPO; and cause the at least one transceiver to transmit the RPO information to the UE. 52. The network entity of claim 51, wherein the at least one RPO is a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs. 53. The network entity of claim 51, wherein the at least one RPO is an RIS not connected to a wireless access network to which the network entity belongs, a mirror, glass, metal, or other reflective object. 54. The network entity of claim 51, wherein the location information for the at least one RPO is a geographic location of the at least one RPO. 55. The network entity of claim 51, wherein: a geographic location of the at least one RPO is not known to the network entity, and the location information for the at least one RPO is an estimated location of the at least one RPO. 56. The network entity of claim 51, wherein: the RPO information further includes an RPO identifier, and the PRS resource location field includes a geographic location of the at least one RPO and the RPO identifier.
